ApplicationArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Lake Formation application integrated with IAM Identity Center.

CatalogId.description: The identifier for the Data Catalog. By default, the account ID. The Data Catalog is the persistent metadata store. It contains database definitions, table definitions, and other control information to manage your Lake Formation environment.

InstanceArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the connection.

ApplicationArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Lake Formation application integrated with IAM Identity Center.

ShareRecipients.description:
A list of Amazon Web Services account IDs or Amazon Web Services organization/organizational unit ARNs that are allowed to access data managed by Lake Formation.

If the ShareRecipients list includes valid values, a resource share is created with the principals you want to have access to the resources as the ShareRecipients.

If the ShareRecipients value is null or the list is empty, no resource share is created.

ServiceIntegrations.description: A list of service integrations for enabling trusted identity propagation with external services such as Redshift.

ResourceShare.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the RAM share.

DataLakePrincipalIdentifier.description: An identifier for the Lake Formation principal.

Identity.description: A unique identifier of the invoking principal.

DataLakeSettings.description: A structure representing a list of Lake Formation principals designated as data lake administrators.

DataLakeAdmins.description: A list of Lake Formation principals. Supported principals are IAM users or IAM roles.

ReadOnlyAdmins.description: A list of Lake Formation principals with only view access to the resources, without the ability to make changes. Supported principals are IAM users or IAM roles.

CreateDatabaseDefaultPermissions.description:
Specifies whether access control on newly created database is managed by Lake Formation permissions or exclusively by IAM permissions.

A null value indicates access control by Lake Formation permissions. A value that assigns ALL to IAM_ALLOWED_PRINCIPALS indicates access control by IAM permissions. This is referred to as the setting "Use only IAM access control," and is for backward compatibility with the Glue permission model implemented by IAM permissions.

The only permitted values are an empty array or an array that contains a single JSON object that grants ALL to IAM_ALLOWED_PRINCIPALS.

For more information, see Changing the Default Security Settings for Your Data Lake.

CreateTableDefaultPermissions.description:
Specifies whether access control on newly created table is managed by Lake Formation permissions or exclusively by IAM permissions.

A null value indicates access control by Lake Formation permissions. A value that assigns ALL to IAM_ALLOWED_PRINCIPALS indicates access control by IAM permissions. This is referred to as the setting "Use only IAM access control," and is for backward compatibility with the Glue permission model implemented by IAM permissions.

The only permitted values are an empty array or an array that contains a single JSON object that grants ALL to IAM_ALLOWED_PRINCIPALS.

For more information, see Changing the Default Security Settings for Your Data Lake.

Parameters.description: A key-value map that provides an additional configuration on your data lake. CROSS_ACCOUNT_VERSION is the key you can configure in the Parameters field. Accepted values for the CrossAccountVersion key are 1, 2, 3, and 4.

TrustedResourceOwners.description:
A list of the resource-owning account IDs that the caller's account can use to share their user access details (user ARNs). The user ARNs can be logged in the resource owner's CloudTrail log.

You may want to specify this property when you are in a high-trust boundary, such as the same team or company.

AllowExternalDataFiltering.description:
Whether to allow Amazon EMR clusters to access data managed by Lake Formation.

If true, you allow Amazon EMR clusters to access data in Amazon S3 locations that are registered with Lake Formation.

If false or null, no Amazon EMR clusters will be able to access data in Amazon S3 locations that are registered with Lake Formation.

For more information, see (Optional) Allow external data filtering.

AllowFullTableExternalDataAccess.description: Whether to allow a third-party query engine to get data access credentials without session tags when a caller has full data access permissions.

ExternalDataFilteringAllowList.description: A list of the account IDs of Amazon Web Services accounts with Amazon EMR clusters that are to perform data filtering.>

AuthorizedSessionTagValueList.description: Lake Formation relies on a privileged process secured by Amazon EMR or the third party integrator to tag the user's role while assuming it. Lake Formation will publish the acceptable key-value pair, for example key = "LakeFormationTrustedCaller" and value = "TRUE" and the third party integrator must properly tag the temporary security credentials that will be used to call Lake Formation's administrative APIs.

NextToken.description: A continuation token, if not all requested data cell filters have been returned.

TableCatalogId.description: The ID of the catalog to which the table belongs.

DatabaseName.description: A database in the Glue Data Catalog.

Name.description: The name given by the user to the data filter cell.

ColumnNames.description: A list of column names and/or nested column attributes. When specifying nested attributes, use a qualified dot (.) delimited format such as "address"."zip". Nested attributes within this list may not exceed a depth of 5.

ColumnWildcard.description:
A wildcard with exclusions.

You must specify either a ColumnNames list or the ColumnWildCard.

ExcludedColumnNames.description: Excludes column names. Any column with this name will be excluded.

VersionId.description: The ID of the data cells filter version.

LFTagExpressions.description: Logical expressions composed of one more LF-Tag key-value pairs.

NextToken.description: A continuation token, if this is not the first call to retrieve this list.

Name.description: The name for saved the LF-Tag expression.

Description.description: A structure that contains information about the LF-Tag expression.

CatalogId.description: The identifier for the Data Catalog. By default, the account ID.

Expression.description: A logical expression composed of one or more LF-Tags.

LFTags.description: A list of LF-tags that the requested has permission to view.

NextToken.description: A continuation token, present if the current list segment is not the last.

CatalogId.description: The identifier for the Data Catalog. By default, the account ID. The Data Catalog is the persistent metadata store. It contains database definitions, table definitions, and other control information to manage your Lake Formation environment.

TagValues.description: A list of possible values an attribute can take.

LakeFormationOptInsInfoList.description: A list of principal-resource pairs that have Lake Formation permissins enforced.

NextToken.description: A continuation token, if this is not the first call to retrieve this list.

Catalog.description: The identifier for the Data Catalog. By default, the account ID. The Data Catalog is the persistent metadata store. It contains database definitions, table definitions, and other control information to manage your Lake Formation environment.

Database.description: The database for the resource. Unique to the Data Catalog. A database is a set of associated table definitions organized into a logical group. You can Grant and Revoke database permissions to a principal.

CatalogId.description: The identifier for the Data Catalog. By default, it is the account ID of the caller.

Name.description: The name of the database resource. Unique to the Data Catalog.

Table.description: The table for the resource. A table is a metadata definition that represents your data. You can Grant and Revoke table privileges to a principal.

CatalogId.description: The identifier for the Data Catalog. By default, it is the account ID of the caller.

DatabaseName.description: The name of the database for the table. Unique to a Data Catalog. A database is a set of associated table definitions organized into a logical group. You can Grant and Revoke database privileges to a principal.

Name.description: The name of the table.

TableWildcard.description:
A wildcard object representing every table under a database.

At least one of TableResource$Name or TableResource$TableWildcard is required.

TableWithColumns.description: The table with columns for the resource. A principal with permissions to this resource can select metadata from the columns of a table in the Data Catalog and the underlying data in Amazon S3.

CatalogId.description: The identifier for the Data Catalog. By default, it is the account ID of the caller.

Name.description: The name of the table resource. A table is a metadata definition that represents your data. You can Grant and Revoke table privileges to a principal.

ColumnNames.description: The list of column names for the table. At least one of ColumnNames or ColumnWildcard is required.

ColumnWildcard.description: A wildcard specified by a ColumnWildcard object. At least one of ColumnNames or ColumnWildcard is required.

ExcludedColumnNames.description: Excludes column names. Any column with this name will be excluded.

DataLocation.description: The location of an Amazon S3 path where permissions are granted or revoked.

CatalogId.description: The identifier for the Data Catalog where the location is registered with Lake Formation. By default, it is the account ID of the caller.

ResourceArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that uniquely identifies the data location resource.

Expression.description: A list of LF-tag conditions or a saved expression that apply to the resource's LF-tag policy.

ExpressionName.description: If provided, permissions are granted to the Data Catalog resources whose assigned LF-Tags match the expression body of the saved expression under the provided ExpressionName.

LFTagExpression.description: LF-Tag expression resource. A logical expression composed of one or more LF-Tag key:value pairs.

CatalogId.description: The identifier for the Data Catalog. By default, the account ID.

Name.description: The name of the LF-Tag expression to grant permissions on.

Condition.description: A Lake Formation condition, which applies to permissions and opt-ins that contain an expression.

Expression.description: An expression written based on the Cedar Policy Language used to match the principal attributes.

Permissions.description: The permissions to be granted or revoked on the resource.

PermissionsWithGrantOption.description: Indicates whether to grant the ability to grant permissions (as a subset of permissions granted).

AdditionalDetails.description: This attribute can be used to return any additional details of PrincipalResourcePermissions. Currently returns only as a RAM resource share ARN.

ResourceShare.description: A resource share ARN for a catalog resource shared through RAM.

LastUpdated.description: The date and time when the resource was last updated.

CatalogId.description: The identifier for the Data Catalog. By default, the account ID. The Data Catalog is the persistent metadata store. It contains database definitions, table definitions, and other control information to manage your Lake Formation environment.

TagValues.description: A list of possible values an attribute can take.

Name.description: The name for the LF-Tag expression.

Description.description: The description with information about the LF-Tag expression.

CatalogId.description: The identifier for the Data Catalog. By default, the account ID in which the LF-Tag expression is saved.

Expression.description: The body of the LF-Tag expression. It is composed of one or more LF-Tag key-value pairs.

TagValues.description:
A list of possible values an attribute can take.

The maximum number of values that can be defined for a LF-Tag is 1000. A single API call supports 50 values. You can use multiple API calls to add more values.

Error.description: An error message when the operation fails.

State.description:
The state of a query previously submitted. The possible states are:

  • PENDING: the query is pending.

  • WORKUNITS_AVAILABLE: some work units are ready for retrieval and execution.

  • FINISHED: the query planning finished successfully, and all work units are ready for retrieval and execution.

  • ERROR: an error occurred with the query, such as an invalid query ID or a backend error.

QuerySubmissionTime.description: The time that the query was submitted.

AverageExecutionTimeMillis.description: The average time the request took to be executed.

DataScannedBytes.description: The amount of data that was scanned in bytes.

LFTagOnDatabase.description: A list of LF-tags applied to a database resource.

LFTagsOnTable.description: A list of LF-tags applied to a table resource.

LFTagsOnColumns.description: A list of LF-tags applied to a column resource.

CatalogId.description: The identifier for the Data Catalog. By default, the account ID. The Data Catalog is the persistent metadata store. It contains database definitions, table definitions, and other control information to manage your Lake Formation environment.

TagValues.description: A list of possible values an attribute can take.

Objects.description: A list of objects organized by partition keys.

NextToken.description: A continuation token indicating whether additional data is available.

AccessKeyId.description: The access key ID for the temporary credentials.

SecretAccessKey.description: The secret key for the temporary credentials.

SessionToken.description: The session token for the temporary credentials.

Expiration.description: The date and time when the temporary credentials expire.

AccessKeyId.description: The access key ID for the temporary credentials.

SecretAccessKey.description: The secret key for the temporary credentials.

SessionToken.description: The session token for the temporary credentials.

Expiration.description: The date and time when the temporary credentials expire.

VendedS3Path.description: The Amazon S3 path for the temporary credentials.

ResultStream.description: Rows returned from the GetWorkUnitResults operation as a stream of Apache Arrow v1.0 messages.

NextToken.description: A continuation token for paginating the returned list of tokens, returned if the current segment of the list is not the last.

QueryId.description: The ID of the plan query operation.

WorkUnitRanges.description: A WorkUnitRangeList object that specifies the valid range of work unit IDs for querying the execution service.

WorkUnitIdMax.description: Defines the maximum work unit ID in the range. The maximum value is inclusive.

WorkUnitIdMin.description: Defines the minimum work unit ID in the range.

WorkUnitToken.description: A work token used to query the execution service.

StorageOptimizerList.description: A list of the storage optimizers associated with a table.

NextToken.description: A continuation token for paginating the returned list of tokens, returned if the current segment of the list is not the last.

StorageOptimizerType.description: The specific type of storage optimizer. The supported value is compaction.

Config.description: A map of the storage optimizer configuration. Currently contains only one key-value pair: is_enabled indicates true or false for acceleration.

ErrorMessage.description:
A message that contains information about any error (if present).

When an acceleration result has an enabled status, the error message is empty.

When an acceleration result has a disabled status, the message describes an error or simply indicates "disabled by the user".

Warnings.description: A message that contains information about any warnings (if present).

LastRunDetails.description: When an acceleration result has an enabled status, contains the details of the last job run.

Failures.description: A list of failures to untag a resource.

CatalogId.description: The identifier for the Data Catalog. By default, the account ID. The Data Catalog is the persistent metadata store. It contains database definitions, table definitions, and other control information to manage your Lake Formation environment.

TagValues.description: A list of possible values an attribute can take.

Error.description: An error that occurred with the attachment or detachment of the LF-tag.

NextToken.description: A continuation token, present if the current list segment is not the last.

DatabaseList.description: A list of databases that meet the LF-tag conditions.

Database.description: A database that has LF-tags attached to it.

CatalogId.description: The identifier for the Data Catalog. By default, it is the account ID of the caller.

Name.description: The name of the database resource. Unique to the Data Catalog.

LFTags.description: A list of LF-tags attached to the database.

NextToken.description: A continuation token, present if the current list segment is not the last. On the first run, if you include a not null (a value) token you can get empty pages.

TableList.description: A list of tables that meet the LF-tag conditions.

Table.description: A table that has LF-tags attached to it.

CatalogId.description: The identifier for the Data Catalog. By default, it is the account ID of the caller.

DatabaseName.description: The name of the database for the table. Unique to a Data Catalog. A database is a set of associated table definitions organized into a logical group. You can Grant and Revoke database privileges to a principal.

Name.description: The name of the table.

TableWildcard.description:
A wildcard object representing every table under a database.

At least one of TableResource$Name or TableResource$TableWildcard is required.

LFTagOnDatabase.description: A list of LF-tags attached to the database where the table resides.

LFTagsOnTable.description: A list of LF-tags attached to the table.

LFTagsOnColumns.description: A list of LF-tags attached to columns in the table.

QueryId.description: The ID of the plan query operation can be used to fetch the actual work unit descriptors that are produced as the result of the operation. The ID is also used to get the query state and as an input to the Execute operation.

Result.description: A response indicating the success of failure of the operation.
